summary: The diff implements all three requirements reasonably well. REQ-1 is covered by backend/main.mo with create, read, and list functions. REQ-2 is covered by the React frontend with player listing, add form, and backend integration. REQ-3 is covered by the dark charcoal/gold theme, industrial patterns, and bold typography. The backend uses name as the key rather than a separate numeric ID, but the getPlayer function fulfills retrieval by identifier. All core requirements appear implemented. A notable hallucinated feature is the updateScore functionality, which was not requested. The frontend/index.css retains default light-mode color tokens alongside the themed frontend/src/index.css, which could technically violate the no generic light-mode styling AC, but the active stylesheet enforces the dark theme.

hallucinated_features.description: Score update functionality (updateScore backend function and frontend UI controls for plus/minus 10 score adjustment on each player row)
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request only asked for create, read, and list functions in the backend, and a frontend that shows name and score. The ability to modify scores post-creation was not requested.
